name: Palacio del Papa Luna by the Sierra de Moncayo — circular from Ólvega
name_original: Palacio del Papa Luna por la Sierra de Moncayo — circular desde Ólvega
region_intro: Road cycling around Ólvega features routes that navigate a landscape characterized by river valleys, such as the Alhama and Añamaza, and the foothills of the Sierra de Moncayo. The terrain includes varied elevations, with significant climbs and descents, making it suitable for experienced road cyclists. The region offers a mix of open roads and routes passing through areas with historical landmarks like Magaña Castle. Best road cycling routes around Ólvega The most popular road cycling route is Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop, a 41.7 miles (67.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Alhama Valley Biosphere, offering varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Moncayo CIMA Climbs Loop, a difficult 38.6 miles (62.1 km) path. This route includes challenging CIMA climbs within the Moncayo region, featuring substantial elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Palacio del Papa Luna by the Sierra de Moncayo — circular from Ólvega, a 73.3 miles (118.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Moncayo, often completed in about 5 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around Ólvega is defined by routes through river valleys, challenging mountain climbs, and scenic roads near historical sites. The network offers options primarily for moderate to advanced ability levels, with 3 moderate and 8 difficult routes available. More than 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ólvega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the general difficulty levels for road cycling routes around Ólvega?
A: Road cycling around Ólvega primarily caters to experienced cyclists. The region offers 3 moderate routes and 8 difficult routes, characterized by varied elevations and significant climbs and descents, especially in the foothills of the Sierra de Moncayo.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Ólvega?
A: While the majority of routes are rated as difficult, there are 3 moderate options available. These routes still feature varied terrain but are less demanding than the more challenging climbs found in the Moncayo region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Ólvega?
A: The terrain around Ólvega is diverse, featuring river valleys like the Alhama and Añamaza, as well as the challenging foothills of the Sierra de Moncayo. You'll encounter a mix of open roads, climbs, and descents, often passing through areas with historical landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Ólvega?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075617240/palacio-del-papa-luna-by-the-sierra-de-moncayo-circular-from-olvega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio del Papa Luna by the Sierra de Moncayo — circular from Ólvega&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 73.3-mile (118.0 km) loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 33.9-mile (54.6 km) route.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the Ólvega area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075617240/palacio-del-papa-luna-by-the-sierra-de-moncayo-circular-from-olvega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio del Papa Luna by the Sierra de Moncayo — circular from Ólvega&lt;/a&gt; spans 73.3 miles (118.0 km). Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075667186/tierra-de-agreda-loop-via-cigudosa-and-anamaza-river-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Ágreda Loop via Cigudosa and Añamaza River Valley&lt;/a&gt;, covering 75.3 miles (121.2 km).

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes around Ólvega?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by notable landmarks. You can explore areas with historical sites like Magaña Castle or enjoy the natural beauty of the Alhama Valley Biosphere. The region also features various mountain peaks and passes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6255470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Madero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for advanced road cyclists near Ólvega?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging ascents, particularly within the Moncayo area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075610431/moncayo-cima-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo CIMA Climbs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.6-mile (62.1 km) route specifically designed for cyclists looking for substantial elevation gain and CIMA-rated climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Ólvega?
A: Given the varied elevations and potential for challenging conditions, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling in Ólvega. Summers can be hot, especially in the open valleys, while winters may bring colder temperatures to higher elevations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Ólvega?
A: Road cyclists using komoot often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to challenging mountain climbs, and the scenic roads that pass near historical sites. The region offers a rewarding experience for those seeking a mix of natural beauty and physical challenge.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Ólvega area?
A: There are 11 distinct road cycling routes available around Ólvega, offering a range of distances and difficulty levels primarily suited for moderate to advanced cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that explore river valleys around Ólvega?
A: Yes, several routes navigate the region's river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077372286/magana-castle-and-alhama-valley-biosphere-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the Alhama Valley, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075667186/tierra-de-agreda-loop-via-cigudosa-and-anamaza-river-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Ágreda Loop via Cigudosa and Añamaza River Valley&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Añamaza River Valley.
tips: Located on the Hidden Face of Moncayo, Beratón offers us wonderful views as soon as we crown its port. The climb from Calcena (where the climb begins) is not very demanding but it is very entertaining.

Located in the Aranda Region and on the Hidden Face of Moncayo, we find this beautiful town of Purujosa where you can breathe tranquility together with the wonderful views of the nature that surrounds it, such as the Moncayo Natural Park. It is recommended to visit the Mirador, the hermitage and its streets.
From this point, on the outskirts of Calcena, you have a good view of the whole town with its traditional houses and its magnificent natural environment.

A charming place with a lot of history is the one that we find perched on top of the rock in the town of Illueca. It is the Castle - Palace of Pope Luna (who was born here) that stands out from afar. They make very interesting guided tours (prior reservation) based on the history of the palace itself and of Papa Luna.
There is restaurant and hotel service
